name: two_peers_different_chains
description: Demonstrates the network declaration vocabulary. Two peers are declared — 'honest_peer' and 'adversary' — each with an assigned chain. The harness binds one listener and serves both clients in parallel, attributing each session to the correct imaginary peer. In a complete scenario the two peers would hold divergent chains (different blocks at the same slots); this slice uses the same genesis fixture for both to exercise the vocabulary without requiring a second captured chain.

peers.description: An honest peer serving the canonical genesis chain
An adversarial peer. In a full scenario this would hold a divergent chain; here it uses the same fixture to demonstrate peer attribution.
